decisions.title: Concerns about performance identified
decisions.detail: There are concerns regarding the performance of the dashboard project.

evidence.quote: there are some concerns about performance.

decisions.title: Consider using a better database
decisions.detail: A suggestion was made to potentially use a better database at some point for the project.

evidence.quote: Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.

decisions.title: UI design needs improvement
decisions.detail: The User Interface needs to be improved to look nicer.

evidence.quote: The UI needs to look nicer.

decisions.title: Current UI design is not satisfactory
decisions.detail: It was agreed by attendees that the current design of the UI is not great.

evidence.quote: We all agreed the current design isn't great.

decisions.title: Timelines for project remain undecided
decisions.detail: Discussions were held regarding project timelines, but no firm decisions were made.

evidence.quote: There was a discussion about timelines but nothing was firmly decided.

decisions.title: CRM integration timing and method is unclear
decisions.detail: There is a potential future need to integrate with the CRM system, but the timing and method for this integration are currently uncertain.

evidence.quote: We might need to integrate with the CRM system eventually, but we're not sure when or how.

decisions.title: Budget identified as a concern
decisions.detail: Budget was briefly mentioned and is a concern, but no specific figures or details were shared.

evidence.quote: Budget was mentioned briefly — it's a concern but no numbers were shared.

decisions.title: Testing needs to be more thorough
decisions.detail: The team lead indicated that testing practices should be more thorough going forward.

evidence.quote: The team lead hinted that testing should be more thorough.

decisions.title: Improved documentation is needed
decisions.detail: It was suggested that documentation practices should be improved for future work.

evidence.quote: We should probably document things better going forward.

implementation_scope: Performance optimization of the dashboard project.
Refactor/reimplement dashboard UI components to align with improved design specifications.
Implement more thorough testing practices and potentially enhance test coverage for existing/new features.
Improve documentation for existing and future codebase components, APIs, and deployment processes.
technical_constraints: Consideration of using a better database system at some point.
open_technical_questions: Determine the timing and specific integration method for the CRM system.

unresolved_behaviors: Specific performance metrics and acceptable thresholds for the dashboard project are not defined.
The criteria for 'nicer' UI design are subjective; specific design mockups or objective standards are required to validate UI improvements.
Project timelines and key delivery milestones remain undecided.
The timing and specific method for potential CRM system integration are unclear and require definition.

decision_history: It was agreed by attendees that the current design of the User Interface is not satisfactory, necessitating improvement (D004).
It was decided that the User Interface needs to be improved to look nicer (D003).
The team lead indicated that testing practices should be more thorough going forward (D008).
It was suggested and implicitly agreed that documentation practices should be improved for future work (D009).
dependencies: Potential future integration with the CRM system (inferred, timing and method unclear) (D006).
risks: Confirmed concern regarding the performance of the dashboard project (D001). (Likelihood: High, Impact: High - could affect user adoption and stability).
Budget has been identified as a concern, but no specific figures or details were shared (D007). (Likelihood: High, Impact: High - could significantly impact scope, resources, and timeline).

The current database may be insufficient, with a suggestion to use a 'better database at some point' (D002). This represents a potential performance bottleneck or technical debt if not addressed. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium-High - could affect system stability and future scalability).
alignment_gaps: Project timelines remain undecided (D005) – stakeholders may have differing expectations on delivery dates.
The timing and method for potential CRM system integration are unclear (D006), leading to an alignment gap on project scope and roadmap.
The scope and specific requirements for UI improvements (D003, D004) are not explicitly defined, potentially leading to differing expectations on what 'nicer' means.
open_questions: What are the firm timelines and key milestones for the project? (High Impact - D005)
When and how will the potential integration with the CRM system occur, and what is its scope? (High Impact - D006)
What specific improvements are required for the UI design, and what is the scope and budget for this redesign effort? (High Impact - D003, D004)
What are the specific budget figures and constraints for this project? (High Impact - D007)
What specific actions, processes, or resources are needed to ensure more thorough testing practices? (Medium Impact - D008)
What specific improvements are needed for documentation practices, and how will they be implemented? (Medium Impact - D009)
Is the current database truly inadequate, and if so, what 'better' database solution is recommended, and when should this transition occur? (Medium-High Impact - D002)

system_requirements: The dashboard project SHALL meet defined performance targets (inferred — needs confirmation).
The system UI SHALL be improved to enhance user experience and aesthetics.
The system SHALL be subject to thorough testing prior to release and during maintenance cycles (inferred — needs confirmation).
The system's technical design, implementation, and operational aspects SHALL be comprehensively documented (inferred — needs confirmation).
data_flows: Potential future data flow: Data exchange between the dashboard project and an external CRM system (timing and method unclear — needs confirmation).
interface_contracts: Potential future contract: API specifications for integrating with the CRM system (inferred — needs confirmation).
constraints_and_assumptions: The underlying database technology for the dashboard project SHOULD be evaluated for potential improvement or replacement.
open_analysis_questions: What are the specific performance targets for the dashboard project (e.g., response times, throughput, concurrent users)?
What is the scope, timing, and technical approach for integrating with the CRM system? Which specific CRM system? What data elements will be exchanged, in what direction, and with what frequency?
What are the detailed functional and non-functional requirements for the improved UI design?
What are the criteria and scope for 'thorough testing practices'?
What are the scope and standards for 'improved documentation' for the system?
What are the project timelines and key milestones for the dashboard development?
What are the budgetary constraints that might influence technical decisions or scope?

stakeholder.business_summary: The team has identified significant concerns regarding the dashboard project's performance and its current user interface, which was agreed to be unsatisfactory. Key decisions on project timelines, budget, and integration with our CRM system are still pending and require stakeholder input to move forward.
key_impact: The current poor user interface will negatively impact user satisfaction and adoption of the dashboard.
Concerns about performance could lead to a slow and frustrating experience for users.
Without clear timelines and an approved budget, the project faces uncertainty regarding completion dates and resource allocation.
Potential quality issues and higher future maintenance costs could arise if testing is not made more thorough and documentation is not improved.
decisions_needing_alignment: Project timelines need to be established to set clear expectations for delivery.
Specific budget details and approval are required to ensure the project has the necessary resources.
The timing and method for integrating with our CRM system need to be determined to plan future development effectively.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the confirmed timelines for the project's completion and key milestones?
What is the approved budget for this project, and how will it address the identified performance and design improvements?
When and how will the dashboard integrate with our existing CRM system?

issues.description: The 'acceptance_criteria', 'test_scenarios', and 'edge_cases' sections are empty, making the QA view vague and less actionable for its intended audience.
issues.suggestion: Populate these sections with relevant, specific criteria, scenarios, and edge cases derived from the extracted decisions, even if they are high-level or identified as needing further definition. For example, 'UI design improvement - Acceptance Criteria: Needs specific design mockups or objective standards (D003, D004).'

issues.description: The 'milestones' section is empty, which is a critical omission for a Project Manager's report on project status and future planning.
issues.suggestion: Even if timelines are undecided (D005), the PM view should reflect the need for milestones, perhaps listing them as 'To Be Defined' or proposing a process for their establishment.

issues.description: Decision D006 (CRM integration) is listed as '(inferred, timing and method unclear)' in the dependencies. D006 is a 'confirmed' 'open_question' in the extracted decisions.
issues.suggestion: While the timing and method are indeed unclear, the potential need for integration is 'confirmed'. The 'inferred' tag for the existence of the dependency is less precise. Consider rephrasing to 'Potential future integration with the CRM system (confirmed open question, timing and method unclear) (D006)' to accurately reflect the confidence of the original decision.

issues.description: The system requirements derived from D008 ('The system SHALL be subject to thorough testing...') and D009 ('The system's technical design... SHALL be comprehensively documented...') are both marked as '(inferred — needs confirmation)'. However, D008 ('Testing needs to be more thorough') and D009 ('Improved documentation is needed') are confirmed functional requirements.
issues.suggestion: The *need* for thorough testing and improved documentation is confirmed. What needs confirmation are the specific *details*, *scope*, and *implementation* of these requirements (e.g., 'What are the criteria for 'thorough' testing?'). The 'inferred' tag should clarify that it applies to the detailed 'SHALL' statement and its scope, not to the underlying confirmed requirement itself.

review.reviewer_notes: The report generally aligns with extracted decisions and provides useful role-specific information. However, the QA and PM views have critical sections (acceptance criteria, test scenarios, milestones) that are empty, reducing their utility. The SA view also has minor inaccuracies in how it labels confirmed requirements as 'inferred' when only the details are truly inferred.

evidence_quotes: there are some concerns about performance.
Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.
The UI needs to look nicer.
